summary: Sahi, a Bengaluru-based stock trading platform, raised $33 million in a Series B round led by Accel with participation from Elevation Capital to expand its AI-driven technology and product suite.
Kovon, a cross-border workforce mobility platform, raised $250K in a pre-seed round led by TDV Partners to develop its product, expand employer partnerships and build a skilling ecosystem.
HyugaLife, a marketplace for proteins and supplements, raised Rs 100 crore (≈$10.5 million) in a Series A round led by IvyCap Ventures with participation from First Bridge Fund.
House of Chikankari raised Rs 25 crore in a Series A round led by Cap Alpha Ventures to expand its product portfolio, offline retail presence, and marketing.

investors: Alteria Capital | Cornerstone Ventures | ESV-Arthya AIF | Innoven Capital | Roots Ventures | Shepherd’s Hill Private Equity
summary: Novio, a fintech startup offering credit cards backed by fixed deposits, raised Rs 100 crore in a Series A round led by Cornerstone Ventures, with participation from several investors and venture debt providers.

investors: Blume Ventures | Hardik Pandya | Jasprit Bumrah | MIXI | MS Dhoni | Ravindra Jadeja | Sai Sudharsan | Shreyas Iyer | Tilak Varma | Times Internet | V3 Ventures
summary: LightFury Games raised $11 million in a pre‑Series A round from investors including Blume, V3 Ventures, MIXI, Times Internet and several Indian cricketers, to fund development and live‑ops of its AAA mobile cricket title eCricket.

investors: InfoEdge Ventures | Kae Capital | Susquehanna Venture Capital
summary: GobbleCube raised $15 million in a Series A round backed by Susquehanna Venture Capital.

investors: ITI Growth Opportunities Fund | Merisis Wealth Trust | PROMAFT Partners | V3 Ventures
summary: The Hosteller raised Rs 150 crore in a Series B round led by PROMAFT Partners and V3 Ventures, with participation from ITI Growth Opportunities Fund and Merisis Wealth Trust, to expand its hostel network and launch a travel super app.

TraqCheck, an HR tech startup, raised $8 million in a Series A round led by IvyCap Ventures with participation from IIFL Fintech Fund to expand its AI-driven background screening and sourcing agents in Europe.

Pluckk, a digital-first fresh food and clean-label FMCG brand, raised Rs 100 crore in a Series A1 round led by Euro Gulf Investment to expand offline retail, international markets, and AI-driven supply chain capabilities.

investors: A.P. Moller Holding | Advent International | Dragon Funds | Hornbill Capital | Motilal Oswal Alternates | Premji Invest | WhiteOak Capital
summary: Fintech startup KreditBee raised $280 million from a consortium of investors including Motilal Oswal Alternates, Hornbill Capital, Dragon Funds, WhiteOak Capital, A.P. Moller Holding, Premji Invest and Advent International.

investors: Anicut Capital | Capital A | Garima Vohra | Gaurav Chanana | IAN Alpha Fund | Let’s Venture | Succeed Innovation
summary: SportVot, a Mumbai-based sports-tech startup, raised Rs 32.7 crore in a funding round led by IAN Alpha Fund, with participation from several investors, to expand internationally and enhance its AI-driven production capabilities.
IndiGo Ventures Fund invested approximately Rs 10 crore in Sarla Aviation, an eVTOL air-taxi startup, to support its operating and expansion needs.
The article reports JioHotstar's FY26 revenue, profit before tax and operational metrics, without mentioning any funding round.
Peak XV Partners sold its 7.7% stake in fintech company MobiKwik in a block deal worth about Rs 130 crore.
Unicommerce reported Q4 FY26 revenue of Rs 52.8 crore and profit of Rs 3.4 crore, with a 70% YoY revenue rise in Q3 and a market cap of about $133 million.
The government announced a Rs 10,000 crore Fund of Funds 2.0 to support deep‑tech, manufacturing and early‑growth startups through segmented AIFs.
Eternal, a foodtech and quick commerce platform, reported a 4.5X profit increase to Rs 174 crore in Q4 FY26, with revenue of Rs 17,634 crore.
Go Digit General Insurance reported a 28% year‑on‑year profit increase to Rs 149 crore in Q4 FY26, with flat revenue and higher net premiums.
Bluestone, an omnichannel jewellery retailer, reported a 48% YoY revenue increase to Rs 681 cr in Q4 FY26 and posted a profit of Rs 31 cr.
